title: CI/CD Detection and Integration
description: Automatically detect CI/CD environments and adjust Jarvy behavior for non-interactive, headless execution in pipelines
acceptance_criteria: Auto-detect 8+ CI providers (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI, etc.)
Skip all interactive prompts in CI mode
Use sensible defaults instead of prompting
CI config generation for major providers
Same jarvy.toml works locally and in CI
Clear exit codes for pipeline success/failure

tasks.title: Create CI detection module
tasks.description: Create src/ci.rs with provider detection logic
subtasks: Create CiProvider enum with all supported providers
Implement detect() method checking environment variables
Add is_ci() helper method
Add supports_groups() for providers with log grouping
Add cache_dir() for provider-specific cache locations
Write unit tests with mocked env vars

tasks.title: Implement CI output formatting
subtasks: Create CiOutput struct with provider field
Implement group_start() with provider-specific syntax
Implement group_end() with provider-specific syntax
Implement warning() with provider formatting
Implement error() with provider formatting
Implement set_output() for GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps

tasks.title: Modify prompts for CI mode
tasks.description: Update prompt functions to auto-answer in CI
subtasks: Modify confirm() to return default in CI mode
Modify select() to return default in CI mode
Log auto-answered prompts for debugging
Ensure no blocking stdin reads in CI

tasks.title: Add CI section to config
subtasks: Add CiConfig struct with skip_tools, tools override, env
Add CiSettings struct with no_color, no_progress
Implement config merging for CI overrides
Write unit tests for CI config parsing

tasks.title: Add CI-specific tool installation
tasks.description: Optimize tool installation for CI environments
subtasks: Check for CI cache directories
Use minimal profiles for tools like rustup in CI
Skip interactive configuration steps
Use pinned versions for reproducibility

tasks.title: Add CLI flags for CI control
tasks.description: Add --ci and --no-ci flags to override detection
subtasks: Add --ci flag to force CI mode
Add --no-ci flag to force interactive mode
Update setup command to respect flags
Add help text for new flags

tasks.title: Implement CI config generation
tasks.description: Add jarvy ci-config command to generate CI workflows
subtasks: Create template for GitHub Actions workflow
Create template for GitLab CI configuration
Create template for CircleCI configuration
Create template for Azure DevOps pipeline
Add jarvy ci-config <provider> command

tasks.title: Update all interactive code paths
tasks.description: Ensure all interactive features respect CI mode
subtasks: Audit all uses of inquire/dialoguer
Replace with CI-aware wrappers
Disable spinners and progress bars in CI
Use plain text output instead of colors (optional)

tasks.title: Write integration tests
tasks.description: Test CI behavior with mocked environments
subtasks: Test GitHub Actions detection
Test GitLab CI detection
Test generic CI=true detection
Test auto-answered prompts
Test --ci and --no-ci flags
Test output formatting per provider

tasks.title: Document CI integration
subtasks: Document auto-detection behavior
Add examples for GitHub Actions
Add examples for GitLab CI
Document caching strategies
Document CI config generation

implementation_notes.detection_order: Check specific providers first, generic CI=true last
implementation_notes.no_new_deps: Uses only environment variable detection, no new dependencies
implementation_notes.backwards_compatible: Same jarvy.toml works unchanged in CI
implementation_notes.exit_codes: Use consistent exit codes for CI pipeline status

risks.description: False positive CI detection in non-CI environment
Provider-specific bugs due to different behaviors
risks.mitigation: Test on each major provider, document differences

risks.description: CI behavior may differ unexpectedly from local
risks.mitigation: Document differences clearly, prefer minimal divergence
